title: Register Renaming
summary: Explains RAW, WAR, and WAW data dependencies, and how register renaming eliminates WAR and WAW dependencies by mapping architectural registers to a larger physical register file.
content_points: RAW (true dependency): cannot be eliminated; the written value is genuinely needed.
WAR (anti-dependency): instruction 1 reads a register, instruction 2 writes it. Renaming: give instruction 2 a fresh physical register.
WAW (output dependency): both write to the same register. Renaming: each write goes to a different physical register.
Processor uses more physical registers than the ISA exposes; renaming done automatically.
Increases parallelism; especially effective with superscalar/out-of-order.
learning_objectives: Identify RAW, WAR, and WAW data dependencies in instruction sequences
Explain how register renaming resolves WAR and WAW
